LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PAUL RAYBIN'S MOTION TO SEAL RECORDS FILED CONDITIONALLY UNDER SEAL IS GRANTED, FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. NO OPPOSITION FILED. MOVING PARTY SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH INCLUDES THE EXPRESS FACTUAL FINDINGS REQUIRED BY CRC 2.551(D). THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST OF PERSONAL FINANCIAL PRIVACY SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST IN MOVING PARTY'S FINANCIAL PRIVACY WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORDS ARE NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED, AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. THE COURT ASSUMES THAT THE MOVING PARTY WISHES TO SEAL PORTIONS OF THE REPLY SEPARATE STATEMENT (CONDITIONALLY FILED UNDER SEAL) WHICH ARE IDENTICAL TO THE PORTIONS OF THE SEPARATE STATEMENT FOR WHICH SEALING IS SOUGHT. IF THAT IS THE CASE, THE PROPOSED ORDER TO BE DRAFTED BY THE MOVING PARTY MUST SPECIFICALLY REFER TO THAT DOCUMENT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS DELAGNES LINDER & DUEY LLP and R. MICHAEL DELANGNES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
IS Off calendar. The matter has settled per moving party's request in an email to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Seal Records Filed Conditionally Under Seal
Off Calendar Sep-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF PAUL RAYBIN's Motion To Seal Records Filed Conditionally Under Seal is Granted, for good cause shown. No opposition filed. Moving party shall prepare a proposed order which includes the express factual findings required by CRC 2.551(d). The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; the overriding interest of personal financial privacy supports sealing the record; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest in moving party's financial privacy will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. The Court assumes that the moving party wishes to seal portions of the Reply Separate Statement (conditionally filed under seal) which are identical to the portions of the Separate Statement for which sealing is sought. If that is the case, the proposed order to be drafted by the moving party must specifically refer to that document. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
